what_it_does: Styles the transcript-viz overlay modal: scrim, panel, header, close button, a search input row, status messages, a retry button, and the responsive mount that frames DNA's externally-styled constellation graph.
observations: Overlay uses fixed inset, safe-area padding, opacity/visibility transition, and a max-width 760px centered panel.
A dedicated search row contains a flex-growing pill input and a pill search button, both with a 44px min-height touch target.
The body centers content and adds bottom safe-area padding.
Message states include default, error, and quiet modifiers; a separate retry pill button is provided.
The graph mount overrides .screen.ar to be full-width single-column and disables the .ar-responsive 2-column grid.
The SVG is given a fixed 600px width with max-width: 100% to keep the constellation sized across viewports.
Comments explain the file mirrors the channels/sessions/inbox overlay pattern and that DNA's constellation carries its own styling.
standouts: Explicit override that neutralizes .ar-responsive desktop 2-column grid for the graph mount, preventing the SVG from collapsing.
Search row is called out in comments as the one distinguishing element of this surface.
Heavy reliance on design tokens (custom properties) for spacing, color, type, radius, elevation, and safe areas.
600px authored SVG width combined with a 640px max-width container cap.
conventions: BEM-style class naming with block--modifier variants.
Token-only values via CSS custom properties for colors, spacing, typography, radii, shadows, and safe areas.
Disabled state handled with opacity and cursor change.
Comments written as a design narrative explaining intent and external relationships.
concerns: Close button uses a hardcoded 15px font-size despite the comment claiming tokens only.
Close button has no visible focus style or border-radius, unlike the input and go button.
SVG width is fixed at 600px; on very narrow viewports it relies on max-width: 100%, but the 600px intrinsic width may still affect layout calculations.
The 640px max-width on .screen.ar is slightly larger than the 600px SVG cap, which can leave unused horizontal space.
notes: This file is the CSS shell for the transcript overlay and search row, plus responsive containment rules that keep DNA's constellation graph full-width and single-column.
questions: What JavaScript toggles .transcript-overlay--open and wires the search button?
What markup and classes does DNA's constellation actually emit at runtime?
Where are --safe-t, --safe-b, and the other design tokens defined?
How does this file relate to the channels/sessions/inbox overlay styles it claims to mirror?
Is the 600px SVG width authored here or inherited from DNA's constellation component?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the code: it frames the constellation graph, adds a search row, and prevents the graph from being crushed by a 2-column responsive layout.
apparent_themes: Mobile-first touch targets and safe-area awareness
intention_signals: It is trying to provide a consistent, query-driven transcript visualization overlay that hosts an externally styled constellation graph and keeps that graph centered and full-width on all viewports.
novelty: The deliberate CSS override to suppress a 2-column responsive grid (.ar-responsive) specifically for the graph body, and the framing of an externally styled constellation component within a sibling-overlay modal.
contribution: A self-contained stylesheet defining the transcript overlay shell, search controls, status messages, retry button, and the responsive container rules needed to present DNA's constellation graph at full width without inheriting a multi-column layout.
summary_for_embedding: CSS for a transcript-viz overlay modal including header, search row, status messages, retry button, and full-width responsive container overrides for an embedded DNA constellation graph.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to frame a query-driven transcript visualization overlay that hosts an externally styled constellation graph and keeps it single-column and centered across viewports.
